ISqlHelper.summary: Defines a contract for executing SQL commands against a SQL Server database. Supports ad-hoc text queries and stored procedure execution.

ISqlHelper_ExecuteReader.summary: Executes a SQL command and returns a for streaming rows. The caller is responsible for disposing the reader, which automatically closes the underlying connection.
ISqlHelper_ExecuteReader.param: The SQL query text or stored procedure name to execute.
Specifies how is interpreted. Defaults to for ad-hoc queries. Use for stored procedure calls.
An optional array of objects to bind to the command.
ISqlHelper_ExecuteReader.returns: A instance opened with , ensuring the connection is closed when the reader is disposed.

ISqlHelper_ExecuteNonQuery.summary: Executes a SQL command that does not return a result set (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).
ISqlHelper_ExecuteNonQuery.param: The SQL query text or stored procedure name to execute.
Specifies how is interpreted. Defaults to for ad-hoc queries. Use for stored procedure calls.
An optional array of objects to bind to the command.
ISqlHelper_ExecuteNonQuery.returns: The number of rows affected by the command.

ISqlHelper_ExecuteScalar.summary: Executes a SQL command and returns the first column of the first row in the result set. Useful for aggregate queries such as COUNT, MAX, SUM, or retrieving a newly inserted identity value.
ISqlHelper_ExecuteScalar.param: The SQL query text or stored procedure name to execute.
Specifies how is interpreted. Defaults to for ad-hoc queries. Use for stored procedure calls.
An optional array of objects to bind to the command.
ISqlHelper_ExecuteScalar.returns: The first column of the first row in the result set, or null if the result set is empty.

SqlHelper.summary: Provides a lightweight ADO.NET helper for executing SQL commands against a SQL Server database. Implements the Template Method pattern to centralise connection and command lifecycle management.

remarks.para: This class supports both ad-hoc text queries and stored procedure execution through a unified API by accepting a parameter (defaulting to ).
The default parameterless constructor reads the connection string from the SqlDbConnection entry in the application's configuration file (App.config / Web.config).

SqlHelper_ConnectionString.summary: The connection string used to connect to the SQL Server database.

SqlHelper_Ctor_Default.summary: Initialises a new instance of the class using the SqlDbConnection connection string from the application configuration.
SqlHelper_Ctor_Default.exception: Thrown when the SqlDbConnection connection string is not found in the configuration.

SqlHelper_Ctor_ConnectionString.summary: Initialises a new instance of the class with the specified connection string.
SqlHelper_Ctor_ConnectionString.param: A valid SQL Server connection string. Must not be null.

SqlHelper_CreateCommand.summary: Creates and configures a with the specified parameters. Acts as a factory method to centralise command construction and parameter binding.
SqlHelper_CreateCommand.param: The open or unopened to associate with the command.
The SQL query text or stored procedure name.
Indicates whether is a text query or stored procedure name.
An optional array of objects to bind to the command.
SqlHelper_CreateCommand.returns: A fully configured ready for execution.

SqlHelper_Execute.summary: Template method that manages the full connection and command lifecycle, delegating the actual execution strategy to the delegate. The connection and command are automatically disposed after execution.
SqlHelper_Execute.typeparam: The return type of the SQL operation (e.g. , ).
SqlHelper_Execute.param: The SQL query text or stored procedure name.
Indicates whether is a text query or stored procedure name.
An optional array of objects to bind to the command.
A delegate that receives the configured and returns the result. This is the strategy that varies per execution method (e.g. ExecuteNonQuery, ExecuteScalar).

SqlHelper_ExecuteReader.summary: Executes a SQL command and returns a for streaming rows.
SqlHelper_ExecuteReader.remarks: Unlike other execution methods, this method does not wrap the connection in a using block. Instead, the connection is closed automatically when the returned is disposed, via .

SqlHelper_ExecuteNonQuery.summary: Executes a SQL command that does not return a result set (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). Returns the number of rows affected.

SqlHelper_ExecuteScalar.summary: Executes a SQL command and returns the first column of the first row in the result set.
